current_judgment: First structured evaluation complete. 5-scene eval set run against qwen2.5:14b. All 5 runs returned schema_validation_status: valid. Schema reliability is 1.00. Findings are accurate and non-spurious across all scenes. Dominant style problems (sentence variety failure, adjective overuse, thin description) correctly detected. Key calibration concern: voice_consistency dimension may track tonal register rather than POV fidelity — scene-04 (explicit POV shift) was found in evidence spans but miscategorized to flow/pacing at the score level. Additional POV-shift testing required before baseline adoption.
next_required_decision: Run at least 3 additional POV-shift scenes to probe whether voice_consistency miscategorization (scene-04) is systematic or an edge case. If systematic, determine whether the prompt should clarify voice_consistency semantics. After that investigation, assess readiness for candidate_baseline promotion.
provenance_notes: Metrics derived from operator judgment across 5 eval scenes (run-2026-03-21-001 through run-2026-03-21-005). schema_reliability: 5/5 runs returned valid schema envelope. false_positive_rate: 0 clearly incorrect findings identified across all 5 runs. surface_detection_rate: 4/4 targeted style issues detected (scene-04 counted as detected despite dimension miscategorization). All metrics are operator-judged from a small 5-scene set. Not benchmark-derived. Not gate-eligible. metric_profile is set to detection_reasoning as the closest available value; style analysis identifies issues and reasons about them but is not a binary detection task.
